extra.message: Flask application is running with debug=True and bound to 0.0.0.0. Debug mode enables the Werkzeug debugger which allows arbitrary code execution via the interactive console if a PIN is bypassed. This should never be enabled in production.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: SQL query is built via f-string interpolation of values into INSERT INTO vulns VALUES (...) and executed via conn.execute(). Though these values are static here, the pattern is insecure SQL construction via string formatting.
Hardcoded user credentials ('mike'/'kaines' and 'admin'/'admin') are inserted into the USERS table. Hardcoded credentials can be extracted from source code and used for unauthorized access.

The Host header from the incoming request is interpolated directly into an HTML anchor tag href attribute returned to the client. An attacker can manipulate the Host header to inject arbitrary URLs (host header injection) and perform reflected XSS / open redirect via the unsanitized output.

cwe: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Response Splitting')

extra.message: User-controlled Host header value is reflected into an HTML response ('<a href=...>') without escaping, enabling reflected Cross-Site Scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The 'filename' parameter from user input (lfi.get('filename')) is passed directly to open() without sanitization or path validation. This allows an attacker to read arbitrary files on the server (Local File Inclusion / Path Traversal), e.g., '../../etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-controlled 'imagelink' parameter is passed directly to requests.get() without validation. This allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF): an attacker can make the server fetch arbitrary URLs including internal network resources (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/ metadata, http://localhost/admin). Also disables TLS verification with verify=False.
requests.get() is called with verify=False, disabling TLS certificate validation. This enables man-in-the-middle attacks on HTTPS connections.

User input from sqli.get('username') is concatenated directly into the SQL query string via f-string and passed to cur.execute(). This allows SQL injection, e.g., username=' OR 1=1-- to bypass authentication or extract data.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: SQL exception messages are returned directly to the client in the response body. This reveals internal database schema/errors to attackers, aiding SQL injection exploitation.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: User-supplied 'mathexp' input is concatenated into a Jinja2 Template string and then rendered. This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI), which can lead to remote code execution via payloads like {{ ''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__() }}.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'username' field is reflected back in the response body without sanitization or encoding. When rendered in HTML contexts this can lead to reflected Cross-Site Scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: All API endpoints (including /sqlivuln which handles login/credentials, /lfivuln, /rfivuln, /sstivuln) are exposed without any authentication or authorization requirements defined in the OpenAPI specification. Sensitive operations are accessible to any unauthenticated user.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
